description: Business logic, calculation formulas, and rules that govern how the simulation progresses from one period to the next

calculation_sequence.description: Calculations execute in this order each period

components.base_demand: From config.json market_demand for each product
components.cagr: Compound annual growth rate from config
components.csat_effect: Customer satisfaction multiplier from config.json csat_effects
components.market_conditions: Random market events or competitor actions

market_share_allocation.description: Teams compete for customers based on buying criteria scores
market_share_allocation.formula: Team_Score = Σ(Criterion_Weight × Team_Performance) for all criteria

buying_criteria_scoring.agents: Higher commission = more agent effort = higher score
buying_criteria_scoring.staff_availability: More customer service staff = higher score
buying_criteria_scoring.company_advertising: Higher general advertising spend = higher score

market_share_allocation.weights: From config.json customer_buying_criteria for each product

actual_sales.constraint: Cannot sell more than available finished goods inventory

revenue.by_product: Calculate separately for TechBook, Zroid, iTab
revenue.timing: Revenue recognized when goods are sold

components: Finished goods purchase costs (for units bought from suppliers)

employee_costs.formula: Σ(Employee_Count × REM × (1 + Training_Effect)) for all categories

advertising.formula: Total_Advertising_Budget as set in marketing decisions
advertising.allocation: Split per product and general per allocation percentages

innovation.formula: Total_Innovation_Budget as set in operations decisions
innovation.effect: Increases product feature score over time

distribution.store_costs: Open_Stores × Running_Cost + New_Stores × Setup_Cost + Closed_Stores × Close_Cost

debtors.collection_timing: Based on payment_timing_factors in config.json by payment terms

component_requirements.constraint: Cannot produce without sufficient component inventory

productivity.by_category: Calculate separately for each employee category

lead_time_effects.rule: Supplier lead time affects when purchased goods arrive
lead_time_effects.availability_score: Shorter lead times increase competitive advantage

trade_offs: Higher quality/innovation typically means higher prices
Longer payment terms may come with price premiums
Volume discounts possible at higher capacity levels

calculation.formula: CSAT = f(Quality, Service, Innovation, Availability, Price_Value)

demand_impact.mechanism: CSAT score applies multiplier to base demand
demand_impact.persistence: CSAT changes gradually over time based on sustained performance

relative_performance.description: Teams compete on weighted scoring across multiple dimensions
relative_performance.zero_sum: One team's gain in market share is another's loss
feedback_loops: Higher revenue → More cash → More investment → Better performance
Better CSAT → More demand → Higher revenue
Innovation investment → Better features → Higher CSAT → More demand

strategic_trade_offs.price_vs_quality: Lower prices increase competitiveness but reduce margins
strategic_trade_offs.make_vs_buy: In-house production offers control; outsourcing offers flexibility
strategic_trade_offs.growth_vs_profitability: Market share gains may require margin sacrifice
strategic_trade_offs.short_term_vs_long_term: Innovation pays off over time but costs now
strategic_trade_offs.cash_vs_leverage: Debt provides fuel for growth but increases financial risk

event_system.description: Random events and facilitator interventions can impact gameplay
event_types: Regulatory changes (tax rate changes, labor laws)

facilitator_controls.market_conditions: Can adjust overall market growth rates
facilitator_controls.cost_inflation: Can increase input costs over time

decision_validation.budget_constraints: Total spending cannot exceed available resources
decision_validation.capacity_constraints: Production limited by physical capacity and employees
decision_validation.logical_constraints: Cannot simultaneously raise and retire same security
decision_validation.minimum_requirements: Must maintain minimum cash, employees for operations

error_handling.insufficient_cash: Automatically trigger overdraft or prevent action

implementation_notes: All calculations run sequentially in the order specified
Circular references are resolved through iteration or sequential processing
Cash flow statement reconciles to balance sheet cash
All monetary values use consistent currency units
Percentages stored as decimals internally (e.g., 0.25 for 25%)
Rounding applied consistently at 2 decimal places for currency
Zero-value decisions are valid (e.g., R0 advertising is allowed)
Negative capacity changes (reducing capacity) incur exit costs
Employee counts must be whole numbers (no fractional employees)
